title: poa glauca var. rupicola
intro: Poa glauca var. rupicola, also known as the Rocky Mountain bluegrass, is a variety of the perennial grass Poa glauca. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of western North America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The roots and leaves of Poa glauca var. rupicola have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into a tea or infusion, or dried and used as a powder for medicinal purposes.
